title: sorbus aria var. kovassii
intro: Sorbus aria var. kovassii, also known as the Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white flowers and grey bark. The medicinal properties of Sorbus aria var. kovassii include its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer, as well as its traditional use as a treatment for digestive issues and fever. Sorbus aria var. kovassii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also sometimes used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
